prompt:
Quick one, supposedly: I reworded the usage page skeleton in our doc generator yesterday — pure copy edit, touched nothing but the wording of one template — and now the package doesn't compile:

  ./doc.go:16:35: syntax error: unexpected name snipdoc after top level declaration

There is no declaration anywhere near what I edited, and the word it's stumbling on is just... part of a sentence in the docs? Column 35 of line 16 lands in the middle of prose. I don't get what the compiler thinks it's parsing there.

Whatever you do, the rendered pages must come out byte-identical to the new wording — the wiki styles the backticked bits as inline code, so those have to survive exactly as written. snipdoc_test.go pins the rendered output and the index/TOC behaviour; go test ./... green, test file untouched, and please don't redesign the generator over a copy edit.
